input.headline: Meet Troy - the parrot named after Ireland’s hat-trick hero

raw.headline: Meet Troy - the parrot named after Ireland’s hat-trick hero

winner: Troy the parrot was so proud of his name that he started squawking, "Goal! Goal! Goal!"—then immediately forgot what a goal even is and just mimicked the sound of a crowd cheering, because he hadn’t memorized any actual football yet.

candidates.text: I asked the parrot if he wanted to join the national team, but he just kept screaming about his own hat-trick.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'hat-trick' and ties in the parrot's name with Ireland’s footballer, showing creativity and specificity."}

candidates.text: Troy the parak was so good at mimicking football chants, he scored a hat trick - and now he's got a reputation that's hard to squawk about.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative wordplay with 'squawk about' and 'hat trick,' specific to the parrot and football context, concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: Troy the parrot was so proud of his name that he started squawking, "Goal! Goal! Goal!"—then immediately forgot what a goal even is and just mimicked the sound of a crowd cheering, because he hadn’t memorized any actual football yet.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with the parrot mimicking a crowd, specific reference to football, concise and natural humor."}

candidates.text: Troy is a very proud parrot, but he's constantly disappointed because every time he tries to fly, he just ends up falling over and hitting the grass like a failed hat-trick.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is specific to the headline and uses a clever comparison between the parrot's failed attempts to fly and a failed hat-trick. It’s concise and has a surprising twist, though the humor is somewhat mild and relies on a pun that may not land for all audiences."}

candidates.text: Troy the parrot doesn't squawk about scoring goals — he just sits on the fence and watches the match, occasionally mimicking the referee's whistle.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific, and surprising by connecting the parrot's name to a sports reference in a clever way."}

candidates.text: Why did the Irish parrot get banned from the soccer field? Because he kept squawking about "Troy-ing" around the goal!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat related to the headline but lacks strong surprise and specificity. The pun on 'Troy-ing' is weak and not clearly tied to the parrot's name or the soccer context."}

candidates.text: Troy the parrot squawked out a three-word sentence that made even the local soccer team look like amateurs—it was just 'Ireland, you're on fire... at tax evasion hearings.'
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat specific and includes a clever twist with tax evasion hearings, but the connection to the parrot's name and the soccer team's hat-trick hero isn't fully aligned. The humor is present but could be more tightly focused on the headline elements."}

candidates.text: Troy the par鹉 is now famous for stealing bread, and the police are baffled — he's got a beak for it.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat funny with the pun on 'beak for it,' but the spelling error ('鹉' instead of 'rot') and lack of direct connection to the headline's context (Ireland’s hat-trick hero) reduce its impact."}

candidates.text: Troy is a very impressive parrot, but he still refuses to wear a hat.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat related to the headline but lacks strong surprise or specificity. It's a mild pun on 'hat-trick' without fully leveraging the name connection."}
